schema_note: Each entry pairs the planned figure, the actual outcome, and a programme-level breakdown. Aggregate-only logging is exactly what let the C2 case go unnoticed for two cycles - the total looked fine.

decision_log.note: HAPPY PATH reference: 3 clean cycles, no supplier flags, no capacity overrun, no programme-level shortfall.

flags: Tier 1 programme PRG-002 under-produced by 14%, offset by over-producing Tier 3 PRG-001. Aggregate output (99.8% of plan) hides this.
Same pattern repeats: Tier 1 PRG-002 shortfall widening (18%), masked by Tier 3 over-production. This is now a trend, not a one-off.

EDGE CASE: 3-cycle trend of a Tier 1 (nutritionally critical, clinically tracked) programme being silently under-delivered while the centre reports 99.96% of aggregate target. A volume-only dashboard rates this centre as top-performing. Root cause: shared fortification-line capacity is being allocated to the larger, easier-to-produce Tier 3 programme first.

programme_breakdown.note: Green leafy vegetable shortage from SUP-204 forced a recipe substitution that dropped fortification compliance for ~55% of servings.

flags: EDGE CASE: demand surge (+35% order) exposed SUP-204 under load. Missed vegetable delivery cascaded directly into a Tier 1 programme shortfall. This was the first time this supplier was tested under surge conditions - two prior clean cycles gave no warning.

timeline_of_reversal.event: Production plan finalised: PRG-002 (Anganwadi, Tier 1) and PRG-005 (Pregnant & Lactating, Tier 1) both scheduled to draw from the same 2,000kg fortified-rice stock pool, each planner unaware the other had claimed it.
PRG-002 production line draws 1,400kg fortified rice as planned.
PRG-005 line requests its allocated 900kg fortified rice; only 600kg remains. Shortfall discovered mid-cook, not at planning time.

DECISION REVERSED: PRG-005 batch already partially prepared with non-fortified rice base is scrapped; centre pivots to a reduced-fortification interim recipe pending emergency restock, and escalates to Regional Manager.
Emergency 500kg fortified rice delivery arranged from a neighbouring centre's buffer stock; PRG-005 resumes at 70% of planned volume, 2 hours behind schedule.

decision_log.root_cause: Fortified rice was tracked as a single undifferentiated stock line rather than being reserved per programme at planning time. The ingredient dependency between two Tier 1 recipes sharing one input was never made visible until the physical stock ran out on the line.
flags: EDGE CASE - MID-CYCLE REVERSAL: the value of this data point is not the shortfall itself but that the failure was invisible at planning time and only surfaced through physical depletion. A retrieval system checking 'is there 2,000kg of fortified rice' would have said yes - the actual failure was an allocation/reservation gap between two competing claims on the same input.
Post-incident: centre now manually double-checks shared-ingredient allocation before finalising plan. No systemic fix yet - this is exactly the check the agent should automate.
